title: ISRC Explained
metaDescription: Read about ISRC Explained. Learn how to distribute your music globally, protect copyrights, and maximize streaming royalties with Distrozi.
author: Distrozi Editorial Team

content.text: Navigating the digital music ecosystem requires a clear understanding of how distributors, stores, and registration agencies interact. Correctly preparing your files and metadata is the key to preventing release delays and ensuring you collect every cent your music generates.
An ISRC (International Standard Recording Code) is a unique 12-character alphanumeric key assigned to individual sound recordings. It acts as a digital fingerprint to track streams and sales.
Characters 1-2 represent the country code (e.g. US), characters 3-5 represent the registrant code, characters 6-7 represent the year of registration, and characters 8-12 represent the track number.
Distrozi generates free, official ISRC codes automatically when you upload new releases. Alternatively, you can buy bulk codes from national ISRC agencies.

faq.question: How long does it take for a song to go live on Spotify?
faq.answer: Delivery takes 24-48 hours. However, we suggest scheduling your release at least 14 days in advance to allow for playlist pitching.

faq.question: What is the difference between an ISRC code and a UPC code?
faq.answer: An ISRC code tracks streams and play metrics for individual songs, while a UPC code tracks sales metrics for the overall single or album release product.
